title: Consolidate Documentation Page to Render Support Hub Content
description:
Consolidate the documentation page (`/documentation/`) to render the content from the support page (`/support/`) instead of maintaining separate pages. This reduces content maintenance overhead by keeping a single source of truth for help content.

CSS adjustments were applied to hide the scheduling section (`.schedulesession {display:none;}`) and style the recent blog posts section (`.recentblogs`) to match the overall design.
acceptance_criteria: The /documentation/ page renders the same content as the /support/ page
Only one page needs to be maintained for support/documentation content
The scheduling section is hidden via CSS
Recent blog posts section follows consistent styling
ux_changes: Documentation page now renders support hub content. Scheduling section hidden and blog posts section restyled for consistency.
implementation_notes: Custom CSS classes added: .schedulesession to hide the scheduling block and .recentblogs for styling the blog posts section. Deployed on staging for review before production.
comment_summary: Developer staged the changes and asked for design confirmation. CSS tweaks were applied per feedback to hide scheduling and style blog posts. Final version deployed on staging.
